shapes.name: Text Placeholder 5
shapes.text:
THE SITUATION
UC Davis Health is a leading, nationally ranked academic health system that tackles the world’s most pressing healthcare issues. They provide patient care, research trailblazing therapies, and offer world-class medical education. Their owned digital platform is a collection of academic, clinical, and research department microsites, each with their own client leads and goals. The site architecture and navigation was largely based on internal organizational structure, department initiatives, and leadership requests, creating siloed experiences that made it difficult for their priority audiences, including prospective patients, referring physicians, and prospective residents, to navigate to relevant content easily. They needed a phased and systematic process to overhauling their site, that could get buy-in from across the organization.

Their owned digital platform is a collection of academic, clinical, and research department microsites, each with their own client leads and goals. The site architecture and navigation was largely based on internal organizational structure, department initiatives, and leadership requests, creating siloed experiences that made it difficult for their priority audiences, including prospective patients, referring physicians, and prospective residents, to navigate to relevant content easily. Partnering with their internal marketing team, I took an audience-centered and data-driven approach to re-imagining the UC Davis site network over several phases, ultimately providing the team with a revised design system, new templates, tools, and governance guidelines that they have used to develop and roll out across the site, giving all site editors the ability to create on-brand and engaging content. The process started with multiple rounds of interviews with department heads, followed by deep content, experience, and performance audits, multiple rounds of diagnostic user testing, as well as prototype and tree-testing new designs and navigational models. These critical data inputs were used to inform a system-wide new vision, features, and revised approach to their global taxonomy.

THE SITUATION
Eroxon, the first FDA-cleared over-the-counter (OTC) gel for erectile dysfunction (ED), aimed to establish a strong presence in the U.S. market with its launch scheduled for Q4 2024. My challenge was to develop a pre-launch landing page and a complete website that effectively communicates the product’s unique benefits, educates consumers, and resonates with men experiencing ED as well as their partners. This digital initiative needed to differentiate Eroxon from traditional ED treatments while building trust and engagement within a competitive landscape dominated by oral medications.
THE OPPORTUNITY
The launch of Eroxon in the U.S. market presented a unique opportunity to introduce a revolutionary, non-invasive ED treatment that offers rapid results without the side effects associated with oral medications. Unlike its competitors, Eroxon works within 10 minutes, making it ideal for spontaneous use, and is designed to be discreet and easy to apply. The challenge was to clearly communicate these benefits while addressing common concerns around efficacy and safety, and to engage both users and their partners in a meaningful way.

shapes.name: Text Placeholder 5
shapes.text:
THE SITUATION
Nicorette has been stepping up its game with new gums, chews and lozenges, but their MyQuit program was stuck in the past – literally. Originally designed to help smokers quit, it had evolved haphazardly and piecemeal, becoming a tangled web of outdated applications, clunky websites, and confusing logged in experiences. 
Users found themselves lost in a maze of mismatched designs, redundant content, and overlapping platforms— trying to piece together an experience that simply didn't work anymore. For modern smokers ready to take the leap, the program felt more like a blocker than a helping hand. It was clear: MyQuit needed a serious refresh to meet today’s standards and truly support quitters on their journey.

shapes.name: Text Placeholder 5
shapes.text:
THE APPROACH
After an extensive evaluation of the current Nicorette and MyQuit platforms, I outlined redundancies within the program—from a platform, content, and experience perspective.
Through that evaluation, I documented all content and experiences within the broken platform and outlined what was working, what wasn’t – and what should be migrated over into the new experience. 
I mapped out content and experiences to users at various stages of their quit smoking journey using both existing content as well as new gamification, tracking, and behavioral support tools. This full omnichannel experience, paired with a new visual identity, delivers on the current needs of users looking to quit smoking.

shapes.name: TextBox 11
shapes.text:
INTEGRATION INTO NICORETTE.COM
As FDA standards were adjusted, I quickly determined that the Quit.com microsite, content, and logged-in experience needed to be incorporated into the Nicorette.com site. This absorption allowed one complete, streamlined experience for both cessation support AND product purchase.

A NEW MYQUIT VISUAL EXPERIENCE
With the new launch, I developed a fresh, new brand that not only brought the program into the 21st century, but also paired well with the new Nicorette rebrand that had recently been implemented.

JOURNEY STATES FOR ALL STATES OF SMOKING CESSATION
A smoker’s journey to quit is never linear –I made sure to identify, evaluate, and incorporate content, features, and platforms for all stages of a user’s journey from ready to quit, not quite ready, and even relapse journeys.
TOOLS THAT ACTUALLY WORK
I incorporated new tools into the MyQuit program that didn’t just offer lip service – they actually worked at actively helping a user to quit.

Content Alignment
I meticulously audited, categorized, and re-organized materials from both Nicorette as well as MyQuit. This created an intuitive and smooth content experience, where every piece of content was exactly where it needed to be – delivering clarity and value without confusion.

STREAMLINED PLATFORMS
By identifying redundancies and gaps in platforms and experiences that caused user confusion, I strategically combined platforms – eliminating overlaps, streamlining the experience, and creating a consistent intuitive journey for users.

shapes.name: Text Placeholder 5
shapes.text:
THE SITUATION
With Voltaren transitioning from a prescription-only (RX) product to over-the-counter (OTC) availability, the brand faced a significant challenge: breaking into the highly competitive pain relief market dominated by well-established brands. To achieve high household penetration and increase awareness, Voltaren needed a complete and differentiated digital strategy that deeply resonated with arthritis consumers—a key target audience.
THE OPPORTUNITY
This transition presented a unique opportunity to create a first-of-its-kind digital experience tailored specifically for arthritis patients. By understanding the unique needs of this audience, the brand could build a website that not only provided information but also offered an inclusive, user-friendly environment that addressed the physical challenges faced by arthritis sufferers. This was a chance to set Voltaren apart from the competition by delivering real value to its users through new, accessible features.

shapes.name: Text Placeholder 5
shapes.text:
THE APPROACH
I created a strong omnichannel strategy, inclusive of a pre-launch and full-launch version of the website and CRM program, with first-party data to encourage pre-orders in a first-of-its-kind eCommerce integration. Once in market, I introduced VoltarenGel.com—the first arthritis-friendly digital experience, recognized by The Arthritis Foundation and rigorously tested with real arthritis patients. 
The full site offered brand-new eCommerce functionality and first-party data collection. The site was built with a focus on accessibility, integrating features that were directly informed by the needs of its users. Voice-activated search and tap-to-chat functionalities, large tap targets, strategic page scroll depth, and hands-free content navigation via videos and head gesture scroll education were all critical components. These features made the site not just a digital destination, but a complete support system for arthritis patients.

shapes.name: Text Placeholder 5
shapes.text:
THE SITUATION
Excedrin, a trusted leader in headache and migraine relief, aimed to redefine its digital presence with a complete redesign of Excedrin.com. The goal was to create a user and mobile-first experience that effectively combined product information, head care education, and user engagement. This project needed to push the boundaries of the existing Haleon Web Framework while adhering to the brand’s refreshed Visual Brand Language (VBL).
THE OPPORTUNITY
With the headache and migraine category being both competitive and cluttered, Excedrin sought to solidify its position as the definitive resource for head care. This presented an opportunity to develop a strong digital platform that would not only educate consumers but also drive conversions. The redesign was focused on three core content pillars: full head health education, practical tips, and new product information. Each pillar was designed to increase organic traffic and guide users smoothly from awareness to purchase.

THE APPROACH
The design focused on delivering a user-centric experience with streamlined navigation, making it easy for users to access the information they needed and find the right products. Interactive tools, like the product selector, simplified decision-making, while strategically placed calls to action guided users from awareness to purchase. SEO-optimized content boosted organic traffic, establishing Excedrin.com as the go-to resource for headache and migraine management. This approach differentiated GSK Consumer Health, inspiring similar strategies across brands. 
The result is a modern, engaging site that educates, engages, and drives conversions, cementing Excedrin’s leadership in the head care category.

Not only was the current campaign not gaining the desired momentum and ROI, but it was also dated and geared towards an outdated user group – healthcare providers.
St. Jude had established the need for a refreshed visual look in tandem with a new omnichannel perspective and strategy, in addition to new user audiences, but wasn’t sure where to turn – that’s where my team came in.
